title: Dual pivot mechanical hinge with discreet wiring
abstract: Examples include discreet wiring coupling a physical keyboard to a mobile device. The keyboard includes a user interface component including a first housing body aligned substantially along a first axis, a device interface component including a second housing body aligned substantially along a second axis, and one or more conductors coupling the user interface component to the device interface component. A hinge assembly includes a first hinge body coupled to the first housing body such that the hinge assembly is movably coupled to the user interface component and a second hinge body coupled to the second housing body such that the hinge assembly is movably coupled to the device interface component. The conductors include a first segment extending substantially along the first axis and a second segment extending substantially along the second axis. Aspects enable a user to use a physical keyboard to provide input to the mobile device.
claims:
Claims 1. A detachable keyboard comprising: an input portion comprising a first housing body aligned substantially along a first axis; a device interface comprising a second housing body aligned substantially along a second axis; a hinge assembly comprising a first hinge body and a second hinge body, the first hinge body coupled to the first housing body such that the hinge assembly is movably coupled to the input portion, and the second hinge body coupled to the second housing body such that the hinge assembly is movably coupled to the device interface; and one or more conductors extending between the input portion and the device interface to couple the input portion to the device interface, the conductors comprising at least a first segment extending substantially along the first axis and a second segment extending substantially along the second axis. 2. The detachable keyboard of claim 1, wherein the input portion at least partially defines a first cavity therein, the first hinge body extending within the first cavity such that the hinge assembly is rotatable about the first axis, and wherein the device interface at least partially defines a second cavity therein, the second hinge body extending within the second cavity such that the hinge assembly is rotatable about the second axis. 3. The detachable keyboard of claim 2, wherein the second hinge body comprises an extension member, and the second housing body defines a slot configured to receive the extension member such that the extension member is moveable within the slot as the hinge assembly is rotated about the second axis. 4. The detachable keyboard of any of claim 2 or claim 3, wherein the hinge assembly comprises a first end cap and a second end cap, the first end cap and the second end cap each coupled to the first hinge body such that the first end cap and the second end cap are each configured to rotate about the first axis as the first hinge body is rotated about the first axis. 5. The detachable keyboard of claim 4, wherein the one or more conductors comprise a third segment housed within the first end cap and a fourth segment housed within the second end cap, at least a portion of the first hinge body extending between the first end cap and the second end cap, and at least a portion of the second hinge body extending between the first end cap and the second end cap. 6. The detachable keyboard of any of the preceding claims, wherein the hinge assembly comprises a friction generating mechanism that generates friction when the hinge assembly is rotated about the first axis. 7. The detachable keyboard of any of the preceding claims, wherein the hinge assembly comprises a biasing mechanism that is biased toward an extended position, and the first hinge body defines a notch configured to receive the biasing mechanism such that the hinge assembly is secured in a predetermined orientation relative to the first housing body when the biasing mechanism is positioned within the notch. 8. The detachable keyboard of any of the preceding claims, wherein the first hinge body at least partially defines a third cavity therein, the first segment of the conductors extending within the third cavity, and wherein the second hinge body at least partially defines a fourth cavity therein, the second segment of the conductors extending within the fourth cavity. 9. A hinge assembly comprising: a first tubular body coupled to a first housing body aligned substantially along a first axis such that the first tubular body is rotatable about the first axis, the first tubular body at least partially defining a first cavity; a second tubular body coupled to a second housing body aligned substantially along a second axis such that the second tubular body is rotatable about the second axis, the second tubular body at least partially defining a second cavity; and a conductor extending between the first tubular body and the second tubular body, the conductor comprising at least a first segment extending within the first cavity substantially along the first axis and a second segment extending within the second cavity substantially along the second axis. 10. A method for assembling a detachable keyboard, the method comprising: coupling a first hinge body of a hinge assembly to an input portion such that at least a portion of the first hinge body extends substantially along a first axis within a first housing cavity at least partially defined by the input portion, the first hinge body defining a first hinge cavity; coupling a second hinge body of the hinge assembly to a device interface such that at least a portion of the second hinge body extends substantially along a second axis within a second housing cavity at least partially defined by the device interface, the second hinge body defining a second hinge cavity; and 

 extending a conductor between the input portion and the device interface to couple the input portion to the device interface, the conductor including a first segment extending within the first hinge cavity substantially along the first axis and a second segment extending within the second hinge cavity substantially along the second axis.
